text.title: code-review Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to bring a code-review pipeline to the rules: PR size cap, green CI gate, agentic pre-review, scoped reviewer focus, merge policy.

list.item: Action: add pre-commit / GitHub Action that rejects PRs over 400 lines (excluding lockfiles, generated files, snapshots) unless labeled `large-pr-approved`.
Output: pr-balance.sh wired as a required check.

section.title: Step 2 — Gate review on green CI

list.item: Action: configure branch protection: required checks must pass before "Request changes / Approve" is permitted. PR template links to CI run.
Output: reviewers do not see red-CI PRs in their review queue.
Input: Claude Code / Cursor CI integration.
Action: pre-review job runs on PR open + push. Posts a structured comment: risk callouts, coverage delta, detected anti-patterns.
Output: human reviewer reads the structured comment first.
Action: make ruff / prettier / eslint the source of truth for style. Reviewer checklist focuses on correctness + design.
Output: style debates disappear from reviews.
Action: require >=1 approval (>=2 for `critical` modules), all conversations resolved, all required checks green. Bots may auto-merge dependency bumps after 2-hour soak.
Output: merge gate enforces the rules; rubber-stamping is detectable in audit.
